modules.title: Numerical Methods – 1
topics: Solution of polynomial and transcendental equations using Bisection method
Solution of polynomial and transcendental equations using Newton‑Raphson method
Solution of polynomial and transcendental equations using Regula‑Falsi method
Interpolation using Newton’s forward difference formula
Interpolation using Newton’s backward difference formula
Interpolation with unequal intervals using Newton’s divided difference
Interpolation with unequal intervals using Lagrange’s formula

modules.title: Numerical Methods – 2
topics: Solution of simultaneous linear algebraic equations by Gauss’s elimination
Solution of simultaneous linear algebraic equations by Gauss‑Jordan method
Solution of simultaneous linear algebraic equations by Crout’s method
Solution of simultaneous linear algebraic equations by Jacobi’s method
Solution of simultaneous linear algebraic equations by Gauss‑Seidel method
Solution of simultaneous linear algebraic equations by Relaxation method

modules.title: Numerical Methods – 3
topics: Ordinary differential equations solved using Taylor series method
Fourth‑order Runge‑Kutta method for first‑order equations
Fourth‑order Runge‑Kutta method for second‑order equations
Finite difference solution of two‑dimensional Laplace equation
Finite difference solution of two‑dimensional Poisson equation
Implicit method for one‑dimensional heat equation (Bender‑Schmidt)
Explicit method for one‑dimensional heat equation (Crank‑Nicholson)
Finite difference explicit method for wave equation

modules.title: Transform Calculus
topics: Inverse Laplace Transform by various methods
Evaluation of integrals using Laplace Transform
Solving ODEs using Laplace Transform method

reference_books: P. Kandasamy, K. Thilagavathy, K. Gunavathi, Numerical Methods, S. Chand & Company, 2nd Edition, Reprint 2012.
S.S. Sastry, Introductory methods of numerical analysis, PHI, 4th Edition, 2005.

S. Ross, A First Course in Probability, 6th Ed., Pearson Education India, 2002.
W. Feller, An Introduction to Probability Theory and its Applications, Vol. 1, 3rd Ed., Wiley, 1968.

modules.title: Arithmetic and Logic Unit and Data Representation
Central Processing Unit and Instruction Set

reference_books: M. Morris Mano, “Computer System Architecture”, Pearson.
Dr. M. Usha, T.S. Srikanth, “Computer System Architecture and Organization”, Wiley India.
William Stallings, ”Computer Organization and Architecture”, Pearson.
V. Rajaraman, T. Radhakrishnan, “Computer Organization and Architecture”, PHI.

modules.title: Tree Data Structures and NP‑Completeness
topics: Search and traversal techniques for trees and graphs (in‑order, pre‑order, post‑order, DFS, BFS)

experiments: Write a program for Iterative and Recursive Binary Search.
Write a program for Merge Sort.
Write a program for Quick Sort.
Write a program for Strassen’s Matrix Multiplication.
Write a program for optimal merge patterns.
Write a program for Huffman coding.
Write a program for minimum spanning trees using Kruskal’s algorithm.
Write a program for minimum spanning trees using Prim’s algorithm.
Write a program for single sources shortest path algorithm.
Write a program for Floye‑Warshal algorithm.
Write a program for traveling salesman problem.
Write a program for Hamiltonian cycle problem.
reference_books: Coremen Thomas, Leiserson CE, Rivest RL; Introduction to Algorithms; PHI.
Horowitz & Sahani; Analysis & Design of Algorithm
Dasgupta; algorithms; TMH
Ullmann; Analysis & Design of Algorithm;
Michael T Goodrich, Robarto Tamassia, Algorithm Design, Wiely India

modules.title: Signals and Systems
topics: Block diagram of a communication system
Signal definition and classification (continuous, discrete, deterministic, non‑deterministic, periodic, non‑periodic, energy, power, analog, digital)
Standard signals: DC, sinusoidal, unit‑step, ramp, signum, rectangular pulse, impulse ($\delta$) signal
System definition and classification (linear, non‑linear, time‑variant, time‑invariant, causal, non‑causal, stable, unstable)
Fourier Transform: time‑domain and frequency‑domain representation of signals
Fourier Transform properties and existence conditions
Transforms of gate, unit‑step, constant, impulse, sine and cosine waves
Shifting property of the delta function
Convolution and the time‑ and frequency‑domain convolution theorems

modules.title: Amplitude Modulation
topics: Purpose of modulation and classification of amplitude modulation techniques
Double‑sideband full‑carrier (DSB‑FC) modulation and modulation index
Frequency spectrum of AM wave, linear versus over‑modulation
Power relations and transmission efficiency in AM
Modulation by a complex signal and bandwidth of AM
AM modulators: square‑law and switching modulators, advantages/disadvantages
Suppressed‑carrier AM systems (DSB‑SC, SSB‑SC, VSB‑SC) and comparative analysis
Demodulation techniques: square‑law detector, envelope detector, synchronous detection
AM receivers: TRF and super‑heterodyne architectures
Receiver performance parameters: sensitivity, selectivity, fidelity

modules.title: Angle Modulation
topics: Introduction to angle modulation and its categories
Frequency modulation (FM): frequency deviation, modulation index, deviation ratio, bandwidth requirement of FM wave
Phase modulation (PM) and differences between FM and PM
Direct and indirect methods of FM generation
FM demodulators: slope detector, Foster‑Seeley discriminator, ratio detector

modules.title: Sampling and Pulse Modulation
topics: Sampling of signals and sampling theorem for low‑pass and band‑pass signals
Pulse amplitude modulation (PAM) and time‑division multiplexing (TDM)
Channel bandwidth considerations for PAM‑TDM signals
Types of sampling: instantaneous, natural, flat‑top, aperture effect
Digital signal concepts: quantization, quantization error
Pulse code modulation (PCM) and signal‑to‑noise ratio
Data rate, baud rate, and bit rate calculations
Differential PCM (DPCM), delta modulation (DM) and adaptive delta modulation (ADM)
Comparative analysis of various pulse‑code systems

modules.title: Digital Modulation Techniques
topics: Amplitude shift keying (ASK): generation, detection, equation, bandwidth
Quadrature phase shift keying (QPSK): offset and non‑offset variants

experiments: AM Modulation and Demodulation (Envelope Detector)
Frequency modulation using reactance modulator
Frequency modulation using varactor modulator
Pulse Amplitude Modulation and Demodulation
Pre‑emphasis and De‑emphasis
Analog Multiplexing
Amplitude Modulation using Pspice
Receiver characteristics (selectivity, sensitivity, fidelity)
Operation of Foster‑Seeley loop detector
Operation of ratio detector
reference_books: Singh & Sapre, “Communication Systems”, TMH.
Taub & Schilling, “Principles of Communication Systems”, TMH.
W. Tomasi, “Electronic Communications Systems”, Pearson Education Pvt. Ltd.
Taub & Schilling, “Communication Systems”, TMH.
Abhay Gandhi, “Analog and Digital Communication”, CENGAGE Learning.

modules.title: Basic Concepts
topics: Entities and attributes, entity types, key attributes
Relationships and definition of the E‑R diagram

modules.title: Relational Model
topics: Relational algebra – fundamental operators and syntax
Entity‑Relationship model basics and design process
Extended E‑R features – generalization, specialization, aggregation

modules.title: SQL
topics: Measures of query cost, selection, sorting, join operations
Case studies of ORACLE and DB2

modules.title: Relational Database Design
topics: Functional dependencies – definition, trivial and non‑trivial FD
Closure of FD set, irreducible set of FD

modules.title: Distributed Databases and Big Data
topics: Basic concepts of object‑oriented database systems

experiments: To perform various SQL Commands of DDL, DML, DCL.
Write SQL Commands such as Insertion, deletion and updation for any schema.
To execute Nested Queries, Join Queries, order-by, having clause and string operation.
To perform set operators like Union, Intersect, Minus on a set of tables.
To execute various commands for GROUP functions (avg, count, max, min, Sum).
Write a PL/SQL block for transaction application using Triggers.
Write a DBMS program to prepare report for an application using function.
Designing of various Input screens/Forms.
Create reports using database connectivity of Front end with back end.
Create database Design with normalization and implementing in any application.
reference_books: Korth, Silberschatz, Sudarshan, “Database Concepts”, McGraw Hill.
Elmasri, Navathe, “Fundamentals of Database Systems”, Pearson.
Ivan Bayross, “SQL, PL/SQL the Programming Language of Oracle”, BPB publications.
S. Sharma, J. Agrawal, S. Agrawal, “Advanced Database Management System”, Dreamtech Press.
Leon & Leon, “Fundamental of Data Base Management System”, TMH.

modules.title: Introduction to MATLAB/SciLab
topics: Creating variables, comments and multiline comments
Working with files: scripts and functions

experiments: Write your first Matlab/Scilab program.
Extract an individual element of an array.
Write Matlab/Scilab program to illustrate loops and control statements.
Create a simple plot.
Name the title and axes titles of the plot.
Create a webpage with HTML describing your department using paragraph and list tags; apply various colors, font styling (italics, underline, and two additional fonts), and header tags.
Create a web page using HTML to display a class timetable; use tables to provide layout for a page describing university infrastructure.
reference_books: Fausett L.V. (2007) Applied Numerical Analysis Using MATLAB, 2nd Ed., Pearson Education.
Chapra S.C. and Canale R.P. (2006) Numerical Methods for Engineers, 5th Ed., McGraw Hill.
N.P. Gopalan, "Web Technology", PHI.
Ivan Bayross, "HTML, JavaScript, DHTML and PHP", BPB Publication.

subjects.subject_name: Open Source Software Lab (Linux and R)

modules.title: Introduction to LINUX Operating System
topics: General‑purpose utilities: cal, date, echo, printf, bc, script, passwd, PATH, who, uname, tty, stty, pwd, cd, mkdir, rmdir

modules.title: Handling Files and System Administration
topics: File system navigation and management commands: cat, cp, rm, mv, more, file, ls, wc, pg, cmp, comm, diff, gzip, tar, zip, df, du, mount, umount, chmod
VI editor and security by file permissions (chmod, find, locate)
Compiling C/C++ files and file processing utilities (awk, sed, gcc, sh)
Networking commands: ping, telnet, ftp, arp, rlogin, make, apt‑get
System administration: Linux configuration, connecting to remote machines, adding and removing users

modules.title: Programming in Linux (Bash Shell Scripting)
topics: Script control flow: if‑else, nested if, case statements
Loop control structures: for, while, until, break, continue
Script termination, exit status, sleep and wait
Debugging techniques and executing multiple scripts

modules.title: R Introduction
topics: R reserved words, variables and constants
R programming constructs: for loop, while loop, break & next, repeat loop
R functions: definition, return values, environment and scope

modules.title: R Data Structures and Visualization
topics: R vectors, matrices, lists, and data frames
Object‑oriented features: S3 class, S4 class, reference class, inheritance
Graphical methods: bar plot, histogram, pie chart, box plot, strip chart

reference_books: Forouzan, “Unix & Shell Programming”, Cengage Learning.
Sumitab Das, “Unix Concept & Application”, TMH.
Richard Peterson, “Linux Complete Reference”, TMH.
Michael J. Crawley, “The R Book”, Wiley.
Roger D. Peng, “R Programming for Data Science”, Lean Publishing.
Tilman M. Davies, “The Book of R”, No Starch Press.
